title: Lesson content with at least one citation is accepted
steps: Construct a lesson content document containing a references section with one cited source.
Submit the document to the content module citation validator.
expected_result: The content module accepts the document and returns no citation validation error.

title: Lesson content with no citations is rejected
steps: Construct a lesson content document that contains no references or citations.
Submit the document to the content module citation validator.
expected_result: The content module rejects the document with an error indicating at least one citation is required.

title: Lesson content with an empty citations list is rejected
steps: Construct a lesson content document that contains a references section header but zero citation entries.
Submit the document to the content module citation validator.
expected_result: The content module rejects the document because zero citation entries do not satisfy the minimum-one-citation requirement.

title: Course content document exports to a valid PDF file
preconditions: A course content document exists in the system with known content.
The content module PDF export service is running.
steps: Send a PDF export request for the course content document.
Receive the response body.
Verify the response Content-Type is application/pdf.
Verify the response body begins with the PDF magic bytes %PDF.
expected_result: The content module returns a valid PDF binary whose first bytes are the PDF file signature and whose Content-Type is application/pdf.

title: PDF export of a non-existent document returns an error
preconditions: The content module PDF export service is running.
No document with the requested ID exists in the database.
steps: Send a PDF export request referencing a document ID that does not exist.
Observe the HTTP response status code.
expected_result: The content module returns a 404 Not Found response and does not produce a PDF file.

title: Course content document exports to a standalone HTML file
preconditions: A course content document exists in the system with known content.
The content module HTML export service is running.
steps: Send an HTML export request for the course content document.
Receive the response body.
Verify the response Content-Type is text/html.
Verify the response body contains a complete HTML document structure with html, head, and body tags.
expected_result: The content module returns a self-contained HTML document with a valid structure and Content-Type of text/html.

title: HTML export of a non-existent document returns an error
preconditions: The content module HTML export service is running.
No document with the requested ID exists in the database.
steps: Send an HTML export request referencing a document ID that does not exist.
Observe the HTTP response status code.
expected_result: The content module returns a 404 Not Found response and does not produce an HTML file.

title: HTML export document is standalone and contains all section text
preconditions: A course content document with multiple sections and embedded images exists in the system.
The content module HTML export service is running.
steps: Send an HTML export request for the multi-section document.
Parse the returned HTML.
Verify no external stylesheet or script links are required for rendering.
Verify all section text from the source document appears in the HTML output.
expected_result: The returned HTML file is self-contained: all content is embedded inline and no external resource references are required to render the document.

title: Student submits section feedback after completing a section
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated.
The student has an active course with at least one completed section.
The content module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Send a POST request to the section feedback endpoint with the completed section ID and a non-empty feedback body.
Verify the HTTP response status code.
Query the database to confirm the feedback record was persisted with the correct student ID, section ID, and timestamp.
expected_result: The content module returns 201 Created, and the database contains a feedback row matching the submitted student ID, section ID, and feedback text.

title: Submitting feedback for an incomplete section is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated.
The student has an active course with a section that has not been marked complete.
The content module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Send a POST request to the section feedback endpoint referencing the incomplete section ID.
Observe the HTTP response status code and error body.
expected_result: The content module returns a 422 Unprocessable Entity response indicating feedback cannot be submitted for an incomplete section, and no record is persisted.

title: Submitting empty feedback text is rejected
preconditions: A student account exists and is authenticated.
The student has an active course with at least one completed section.
The content module and PostgreSQL database are running via Docker Compose.
steps: Send a POST request to the section feedback endpoint with the completed section ID and an empty string as the feedback body.
Observe the HTTP response status code.
expected_result: The content module returns a 400 Bad Request response indicating feedback text must not be empty, and no record is persisted.
